page: Microsoft Azure F8amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It is equipped with 945 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also register average performance. For lightweight machine learning, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds and strong prompt processing speeds on small 135M parameter models, though performance drops when processing prompts for large 70B parameter models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F8amds_v7 offers a high-density memory allocation relative to its 8-vCPU compute footprint, making it a resource-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel multi-core scaling. The inclusion of a 945 GB local disk and Premium Storage capability supports data-intensive operations. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F8amds_v7 is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor clocked at 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core operations, database tasks, and memory bandwidth fall in the average range. For machine learning, it delivers top-tier text generation on small models but struggles with larger models. This configuration is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and lightweight inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8amds_v7 is an 8-vCPU memory-intensive virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 64 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage for database and memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8amds_v7 is an 8-vCPU memory-intensive virtual machine with an AMD EPYC processor, 64 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and integrated local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor clocked at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core)
Includes 945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and small-model inference
